REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF REPORT OF REFEREE AND REFEREE'S RECOMMENDATIONS, FOR INSTRUCTIONS TO THE REFEREE, TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE TAX FORMS AND FOR APPROVAL OF REFEREE FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. THE REPORT OF REFEREE IS APPROVED AND ALLOWED IN ALL RESPECTS AS FILED. THE RENT PROCEEDS IN THE AMOUNT OF $117,360.78 ARE DETERMINED TO BE PART OF THE PARTITION ESTATE AND SHALL BE ALLOCATED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE REFEREE'S RECOMMENDATION. THE EXPENSES OF SALE AND PARTITION SHALL BE ALLOCATED TO EACH PARTY ACCORDING TO THEIR RESPECTIVE OWNERSHIP INTEREST. REFEREE IS INSTRUCTED TO PAY DEFENDANT'S COURT-ORDERED ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $17,549.11. THE COURT ORDERS DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE AND RETURN ALL TAX FORMS TO CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF THIS COURT'S ORDER. THE COURT APPROVES PAYMENT OF REFEREE'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $12,155 AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $11.85. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC RULE 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Defendant is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $22,635.00. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Granting Attorneys Fees [Ccp Section 473(B)]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Stephen J. Slocum
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO VACATE ORDER GRANTING ATTORNEY'S FEE

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. The Court does not find excusable neglect under CCP 473(b) in light of the Court's Local Rules regarding E-Service. (See, Local Rule 2.11(A)(6); (Q)(1)-(2).) For the same reason, the Order granting Defendant's motion for attorney fees is not void under CCP 473(d). Finally, the Court notes that Plaintiff's proposed opposition does not challenge that the fees awarded to Defendant were reasonably incurred and meet the statutory standard of mutual interest of the parties. Instead, it puts forth an argument that has already been rejected both by the Referee and this Court. Specifically, this Court has already ordered the costs of partition be allocated according to the interests of the parties, the normal statutory standard under CCP 874.040. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Plaintiffs? Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED in part. Plaintiff is awarded attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $44,523.77. The Court excluded the following costs from the award: (i) Costs incurred from July 30, 2024 to August 20, 2025 as these tasks related to the Trust matter; (ii) costs associated with the Motion to Vacate; (iii) a $12.00 "toll expense" on June 18, 2024. (See, Exhibit A, pgs. 7-8, 19 [April 9, 14, June 18, 2024 billing record].) The Court finds the costs associated with the motion to vacate did not provide a common benefit to all parties. (See, CCP sections 874.010, 874.040.) Likewise, there are no tasks in the billing records or hearings on calendar that correspond with the June 18, 2024 toll expense. These expenses shall be borne by the Plaintiff. Plaintiff's request that the Court allocate 100% of the fees and costs to Defendant's share of the proceeds is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Report Of Referee And Referees Recommendations, For Instructions To The Referee, To Compel Defendant Karen Farac To Complete Tax Foms, And For Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amy Harrington, Partition Referee in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Peter Hadiaris for defendant Karen Farac (via Zoom).
Stephen Slocum for plaintiffs Stephen Farac and Lynda Dowd-Farac (via Zoom).

Mr. Slocum contested the Court's tentative decision. After hearing oral argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF REPORT OF REFEREE AND REFEREE'S RECOMMENDATIONS, FOR INSTRUCTIONS TO THE REFEREE, TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE TAX FORMS AND FOR APPROVAL OF REFEREE FEES AND COSTS is GRANTED. The Report of Referee is approved and allowed in all respects as filed. The rent proceeds in the amount of $117,360.78 are determined to be part of the partition estate and shall be allocated in accordance with the Referee's recommendation. The expenses of sale and partition shall be allocated to each party according to their respective ownership interest. Referee is instructed to pay Defendant's court-ordered attorney's fees in the amount of $17,549.11. The Court orders Defendant to complete and return all tax forms to Chicago Title Company within five days of this court's order. The Court approves payment of Referee's fees in the amount of $12,155 and costs in the amount of $11.85.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
